zh.lifeSummary: 當Ruth Gooch於1627年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾時，她的父親John Gooch27歲，母親Ruth Hammond22歲。她與Peter Weare在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克約克結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1664年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克約克享年37歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ruth Gooch于1627年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾时，她的父亲John Gooch27岁，母亲Ruth Hammond22岁。她与Peter Weare在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克約克结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1664年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克約克享年37岁。

nameMeanings.name: Gooch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Anglia): from the rare Middle English personal name Goche (also found as Joche). It was current in East Anglia from the early 12th to the early 13th centuries as a variant of Anglo-Norman French Go(s)ce, Jo(s)ce, a pet form of Old French Goscelin.
English: alternatively, a nickname from Anglo-Norman French gouge (from Latin gobio), the nominative form of Old French goujon (from Latin gobionem) ‘gudgeon’, perhaps for a gullible person.

Welsh: in southwestern England, possibly an Anglicized form of Welsh coch, goch ‘red(-haired)’, though the sound change is irregular. Compare Gough . It may also be a variant of Cornish and Welsh Couch , with the same meaning.

variants.name: Gough
meanings: Welsh: from coch, goch ‘red(-haired)’. The mutated form goch is normal after a personal name. In England, where the surname occurs from Welsh migration, it may have become hereditary in medieval times but this development was probably much later in Wales. Compare Gooch and Couch , which may sometimes derive from the same source.
Irish (especially Waterford and Dublin): shortened form of   McGough  .

variants.name: Gouge
meanings: English: variant of   Gooch   or   Couch  .

variants.name: Googe
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire and Lincolnshire): variant of   Gooch  .

variants.name: Couch
meanings: English (Cornwall and Devon): nickname from Middle English couch(e) ‘hump, hunch’, used for a hunchback. In Cornwall and Devon it is usually pronounced as Cooch.
English (Cornwall and Devon): variant of   Gooch  .

English (Cornwall and Devon): from Middle English coche, couche ‘bed, couch, tablecloth’ (Old French couche, culche). It could have been a metonymic occupational name for someone who made such items or a nickname for a lazy individual. Alternatively, it could have been applied to someone who produced couchwork, a luxurious embroidery in which gold or silver thread and jewels were sewn into satin or silk robes.

variants.name: Gotch
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh coch, goch ‘red(-haired)’; see Gooch 3.

nameMeanings.name: Ruth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (of uncertain derivation) of a Moabite woman who left her own people to remain with her mother-in-law Naomi, and afterwards became the wife of Boaz and an ancestress of David. Her story is told in the book of the Bible that bears her name. It was used among the Puritans in England in the 16th century, partly because of its association with the English vocabulary word ruth meaning ‘compassion’. It has always been popular as a Jewish name, but is now also widespread among people of many different cultures and creeds.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

Historical Boundaries: 1652: Yorkshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1664: Maine, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1668: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1680: Massachusetts Claim, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1691: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: York, Massachusetts, United States 1820: York, Maine, United States

en.lifeSummary: When Ruth Gooch was born in 1627, in Gloucestershire, England, her father, John Gooch, was 27 and her mother, Ruth Hammond, was 22. She married Peter Weare in York, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1664, in York, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 37.

ko.lifeSummary: Ruth Gooch(이)가 1627년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Gooch(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ruth Hammond(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 요르크 요르크에서 Peter Weare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1664년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 요르크 요르크에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ruth Gooch родилась в 1627 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Gooch было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Ruth Hammond, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Peter Weare в местности ерк, ерк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1664 г. в местности ерк, ерк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 37 лет.
